topic: Sports — Trophies, Tournaments, Venues & Governing Bodies

questions.text: The Ranji Trophy is associated with which sport?
questions.explanation: The Ranji Trophy is India's premier domestic first-class cricket tournament, established in 1934. It is named after Ranjitsinhji (K. S. Ranjitsinhji), the Sussex and England cricketer who was also the Jam Saheb of Nawanagar.

questions.text: India won the first ICC Cricket World Cup (Prudential Cup) in 1983 under the captaincy of:
questions.explanation: India won the 1983 Cricket World Cup (Prudential Cup) under the captaincy of Kapil Dev. India beat the West Indies by 43 runs in the final at Lord's, London. This was India's first World Cup victory.

questions.text: The All England Badminton Championship is the world's oldest badminton tournament. Which Indian won it in 1980?
questions.explanation: Prakash Padukone became the first Indian to win the All England Badminton Championship in 1980. Pullela Gopichand was the second Indian to win it (2001). P. V. Sindhu and Saina Nehwal have won multiple BWF events but not the All England.

questions.text: Which Indian won the first individual Olympic gold medal?
questions.explanation: Abhinav Bindra won India's first individual Olympic gold medal at the 2008 Beijing Olympics in the 10m Air Rifle event. K. D. Jadhav won India's first individual Olympic medal (wrestling bronze, Helsinki 1952), but that was not gold.

questions.text: The oldest football tournament in India is:
questions.explanation: The Durand Cup (1888) is India's oldest football tournament, making it one of the oldest football tournaments in the world. Named after Sir Henry Mortimer Durand, it was originally for military teams. The Santosh Trophy is the national championship but came later.

questions.text: The oldest hockey tournament in Asia is:
questions.explanation: The Murugappa Gold Cup (1895) held in Chennai is the oldest hockey tournament in Asia. The Beighton Cup (also 1895, Kolkata) is of similar vintage. The Sultan Azlan Shah Cup (Malaysia) is prestigious but much more recent. The Aga Khan Cup is held in Mumbai.

questions.text: Viswanathan Anand has won the World Chess Championship how many times?
questions.explanation: Viswanathan Anand has won the World Chess Championship 5 times: 2000, 2007, 2008, 2010, and 2012. He became the first Indian and Asian to win the undisputed world chess title. Magnus Carlsen defeated him in 2013.

questions.text: P. V. Sindhu became the first Indian woman to win the BWF World Championship in which year?
questions.explanation: P. V. Sindhu won the BWF World Badminton Championship in 2019 in Basel, Switzerland, becoming the first Indian woman to win the title. She had finished runner-up in the World Championships twice (2017, 2018) before winning it.

questions.text: The first Asian Games were held in New Delhi in which year?
questions.explanation: The first Asian Games were held in New Delhi in 1951, with India as the host country. India won the most gold medals in the inaugural edition. The second time India hosted the Asian Games was in 1982 (also New Delhi).

questions.text: Mary Kom has won the World Amateur Boxing Championship how many times?
questions.explanation: Mary Kom has won the AIBA Women's World Amateur Boxing Championship 6 times (2002, 2005, 2006, 2008, 2010, 2018), making her the most decorated boxer in the tournament's history. She also won an Olympic bronze at the 2012 London Olympics.

questions.text: India's best-ever performance at the Asian Games was at which edition?
questions.explanation: India achieved its best-ever performance at the 2023 Hangzhou Asian Games (19th edition), winning 107 medals including 28 gold medals. India finished 4th in the overall medal tally.

questions.text: The ICC (International Cricket Council) is headquartered in:
questions.explanation: The ICC (International Cricket Council) moved its headquarters from Lord's, London to Dubai, UAE in 2005 for tax efficiency reasons. ICC has 108 member nations, with 12 Full Members and the rest as associate or affiliate members.

questions.text: National Sports Day in India is observed on August 29 — the birthday of which sports legend?
questions.explanation: National Sports Day is observed on August 29, the birthday of Major Dhyan Chand (1905–1979) — India's greatest hockey player who scored over 400 goals in international matches and helped India win three consecutive Olympic gold medals (1928, 1932, 1936).

questions.text: India hosted the 2010 Commonwealth Games in which city?
questions.explanation: India hosted the XIX Commonwealth Games (2010) in New Delhi — India's first and only time hosting the Commonwealth Games. India finished second in the medal tally with 101 medals (38 gold).

questions.text: The Olympic motto 'Faster, Higher, Stronger' was recently expanded to add which word?
questions.explanation: The Olympic motto 'Citius, Altius, Fortius' (Faster, Higher, Stronger) was officially updated in 2021 to include 'Communiter' (Together), making it 'Faster, Higher, Stronger — Together.' The IOC Executive Board approved this change.

questions.text: BWF (Badminton World Federation) is headquartered in:
questions.explanation: The Badminton World Federation (BWF) is headquartered in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia. Malaysia is a dominant badminton nation. BWF organises all major global badminton competitions including the Thomas Cup, Uber Cup, and World Championships.
